b0cc820b-d8ea-43f7-a924-44523602193e.title:
Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

d49e545d-fd7c-4de8-bd78-a1af069e638e.title: Wikipedia Main Page: Collect 10 linked pages with URL, link text, and concise descriptions

details.extraction_intent: From the Wikipedia Main Page, identify the first 10 distinct anchor links presented in the primary content area, capturing for each: the link URL (href) and the visible link text. For every identified link, visit the target page to produce a concise description (1–2 sentences) of what that page is about. Compile the results as a structured list with fields: url, text, description.
subtasks: Load the Wikipedia Main Page and locate the primary content anchors (excluding navigation menus, footers, and non-content templates).
Select the first 10 unique links and record their URL and link text.
For each of the 10 links, perform a visit to fetch full page content and generate a 1–2 sentence description of the page's topic or purpose.
Assemble the final dataset as an array: {url, text, description} for each link.
Save the collected results in memory and prepare to cite the visited URLs in the final answer.
details.notes: When possible, run the 10 link page visits in parallel to speed up processing. All descriptions should be derived from the actual visited pages to ensure accuracy and enable proper citation of sources.
